yaml:
id: no-public-mutable-collection-val
language: kotlin
severity: warning
rule:
  kind: property_declaration
  all:
    - has:
        kind: variable_declaration
        has:
          kind: user_type
          has:
            kind: type_identifier
            regex: '^(MutableList|MutableMap|MutableSet|MutableCollection|ArrayList|HashMap|LinkedHashMap|HashSet|LinkedHashSet)$'
    - not:
        has:
          kind: visibility_modifier
          regex: '^(private|internal|protected)$'
          stopBy: end
    - inside:
        kind: class_body
    - not:
        inside:
          kind: class_declaration
          stopBy: end
          has:
            kind: modifiers
            has:
              kind: visibility_modifier
              regex: '^(private|internal)$'
note: >-
  Public property exposes a mutable collection (MutableList/MutableMap/...).
  Callers can mutate it behind your back. Expose the read-only interface
  (List/Map/Set) via a public getter backed by a private mutable field.

id: no-var-in-object-declaration
language: kotlin
severity: error
rule:
  kind: property_declaration
  all:
    - has:
        kind: binding_pattern_kind
        regex: '^var$'
    - inside:
        kind: class_body
        inside:
          kind: object_declaration
note: >-
  Mutable `var` inside a top-level/named `object` is process-wide shared
  mutable singleton state — a data race and a hidden global. Model state
  with a StateFlow/Mutex-guarded holder or pass it explicitly, not via a
  mutable singleton field.

id: no-volatile-var
language: kotlin
severity: warning
rule:
  kind: property_declaration
  all:
    - has:
        kind: binding_pattern_kind
        regex: '^var$'
    - has:
        kind: modifiers
        has:
          kind: annotation
          has:
            kind: user_type
            has:
              kind: type_identifier
              regex: '^Volatile$'
note: >-
  @Volatile var is hand-rolled cross-thread shared mutable state. Prefer a
  StateFlow (for observable state) or a Mutex-guarded holder (for
  invariants) so reads/writes compose and stay race-free. If @Volatile is
  genuinely the right primitive, keep it private and document why.
goodExample:
private val _currentJob = MutableStateFlow<Job?>(null)
val currentJob: StateFlow<Job?> = _currentJob.asStateFlow()

yaml:
id: no-public-mutable-collection-ctor-prop
language: kotlin
severity: warning
rule:
  kind: class_parameter
  all:
    - has:
        kind: binding_pattern_kind
    - has:
        kind: user_type
        has:
          kind: type_identifier
          regex: '^(MutableList|MutableMap|MutableSet|MutableCollection|ArrayList|HashMap|LinkedHashMap|HashSet|LinkedHashSet)$'
    - not:
        has:
          kind: visibility_modifier
          regex: '^(private|internal|protected)$'
          stopBy: end
    - inside:
        kind: primary_constructor
    - not:
        inside:
          kind: class_declaration
          stopBy: end
          has:
            kind: modifiers
            has:
              kind: visibility_modifier
              regex: '^(private|internal)$'
note: >-
  Public constructor property of a public class exposes a mutable
  collection. Take a read-only collection (List/Map/Set) in the
  constructor and keep any mutable copy private to the class.

id: no-loop-in-init
language: kotlin
severity: warning
message: Imperative loop inside an init block. Move this construction logic into a factory function or compute it from typed members.
note: |
  An init block with a for/while loop that reassigns a `var` is non-trivial
  construction logic (here: building a middleware/decorator chain by mutating a
  `var` in a loop). It is hard to test in isolation and hides construction behind
  object creation. Prefer a private/companion factory function that returns the
  built value, then assign it: `val chain = buildChain(inner, middlewares)`.
  Tenet: explicit construction over magic; clean composable testable units.
  (require()/check() validation init blocks — including per-element validation
  loops that only call require/check — do NOT match this rule and are encouraged.)
rule:
  kind: anonymous_initializer
  has:
    stopBy: end
    any:
      - kind: for_statement
      - kind: while_statement
      - kind: do_while_statement
    has:
      stopBy: end
      kind: assignment

id: no-callback-wiring-in-init
language: kotlin
severity: warning
message: Registering a lambda on a collaborator inside init leaks `this` before construction completes.
note: |
  Assigning a lambda to a property of a constructor-supplied collaborator during
  init (e.g. `transport.onClose = { onMessage(it) }`) hands the still-constructing
  object's behavior to another object before the constructor returns. If that
  collaborator invokes the callback early (or on another thread), it observes a
  half-initialized `this`. Move wiring into an explicit `start()`/`connect()`
  method called after construction. Tenet: explicit construction over magic.
  Note: `this.foo = { ... }` and bare `foo = { ... }` (own-field init) do NOT match.
  Caveat: assigning a lambda to a property of a LOCAL object built inside init
  (a builder pattern) is not a leak; treat such a warning as a false positive.
rule:
  kind: anonymous_initializer
  has:
    stopBy: end
    kind: assignment
    all:
      - has:
          kind: directly_assignable_expression
          all:
            - has:
                kind: navigation_suffix
            - not:
                has:
                  kind: this_expression
      - has:
          kind: lambda_literal

id: no-try-catch-in-init
language: kotlin
severity: warning
message: try/catch inside an init block performs fallible work during construction and tends to swallow failures.
note: |
  A try/catch executed directly in init means the constructor is doing work that
  can fail (I/O, parsing, decoding) and is deciding how to recover at construction
  time — often by fabricating a fallback (`catch { "" }`), which violates the
  no-sentinel rule. Construction should be total and cheap; move fallible work
  into a suspend factory that can fail explicitly or return a typed result.
  Tenet: explicit construction over magic; no sentinel fabrication.
  (A try/catch inside a lambda/callback defined in init runs at call time, not
  construction time, and is intentionally NOT matched.)
rule:
  kind: try_expression
  inside:
    kind: anonymous_initializer
    stopBy: end
  not:
    inside:
      any:
        - kind: lambda_literal
        - kind: anonymous_function
      stopBy: end
goodExample:
class Config private constructor(val data: String)
suspend fun loadConfig(path: String): Config = Config(readFile(path)) // failure propagates

yaml:
id: no-any-typed-public-property
language: kotlin
severity: warning
rule:
  all:
    - kind: type_identifier
      regex: '^Any$'
    - inside:
        kind: user_type
        inside:
          any:
            - kind: class_parameter
            - kind: variable_declaration
            - kind: nullable_type
              inside:
                any:
                  - kind: class_parameter
                  - kind: variable_declaration
    - inside:
        stopBy: end
        any:
          - kind: class_parameter
            all:
              - has: { kind: binding_pattern_kind }
              - not: { has: { kind: modifiers, has: { kind: visibility_modifier, regex: 'private|internal|protected' } } }
              - inside:
                  kind: primary_constructor
                  inside:
                    kind: class_declaration
                    not: { has: { kind: modifiers, has: { kind: visibility_modifier, regex: 'private|internal' } } }
          - kind: property_declaration
            all:
              - has: { kind: binding_pattern_kind }
              - not: { has: { kind: modifiers, has: { kind: visibility_modifier, regex: 'private|internal|protected' } } }
              - inside:
                  kind: class_body
                  inside:
                    kind: class_declaration
                    not: { has: { kind: modifiers, has: { kind: visibility_modifier, regex: 'private|internal' } } }
badExample: public class APICallError(public val data: Any? = null)
goodExample: public class APICallError(public val data: JsonElement? = null)

yaml:
id: no-mutable-var-in-enum
language: kotlin
severity: error
rule:
  kind: property_declaration
  has:
    kind: binding_pattern_kind
    regex: '^var$'
  inside:
    kind: enum_class_body
badExample: enum class Mode { A, B; var current: Int = 0 }
goodExample: enum class Mode { A, B }  // carry mutable state outside the enum

yaml:
id: no-else-in-sealed-when
language: kotlin
severity: warning
rule:
  kind: when_expression
  all:
    - has: { kind: when_subject }
    - has: { kind: when_entry, regex: '^else\b' }
    - has:
        kind: when_entry
        has:
          kind: when_condition
          has:
            kind: type_test
            has:
              kind: user_type
              regex: '^[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*\.[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*$'
badExample: when (event) { is StreamEvent.TextDelta -> a(); is StreamEvent.TextEnd -> b(); else -> Unit }
goodExample: when (event) { is StreamEvent.TextDelta -> a(); is StreamEvent.TextEnd -> b(); is StreamEvent.StreamStart -> c() } // exhaustive, no else

yaml:
id: no-multiple-boolean-params
language: kotlin
severity: warning
rule:
  kind: parameter
  has:
    kind: user_type
    has: { kind: type_identifier, regex: '^Boolean$' }
  follows:
    kind: parameter
    stopBy: end
    has:
      kind: user_type
      has: { kind: type_identifier, regex: '^Boolean$' }
goodExample: fun configure(options: Set<ConfigFlag>) {}  // or distinct enum/value-class params

yaml:
id: no-generate-id-sentinel
language: kotlin
severity: error
rule:
  pattern: "$X ?: generateId($$$ARGS)"
goodExample: toolCallId = WireDecoder.requiredString(callObj, "id", provider, "chat completion response", path)

yaml:
id: no-uuid-id-sentinel
language: kotlin
severity: error
rule:
  any:
    - pattern: "$X ?: Uuid.random()"
    - pattern: "$X ?: Uuid.random().toString()"
    - pattern: "$X ?: randomUUID()"
    - pattern: "$X ?: $C.randomUUID()"
    - pattern: "$X ?: $C.randomUUID().toString()"
goodExample: val id = WireDecoder.requiredString(obj, "id", provider, operation, path)

yaml:
id: no-globalscope
language: kotlin
severity: error
message: "GlobalScope is unstructured concurrency — coroutines launched here outlive every caller and cannot be cancelled."
note: "Inject a CoroutineScope (or use coroutineScope { }/supervisorScope { }) so work is bound to a lifecycle."
rule:
  kind: simple_identifier
  regex: '^GlobalScope$'
  not:
    inside:
      kind: import_header
      stopBy: end

id: no-runblocking-in-common
language: kotlin
severity: error
message: "runBlocking blocks the calling thread and is unavailable on JS/wasm targets — it does not belong in commonMain."
note: "Expose a suspend function instead; let the caller bridge to blocking code at the platform edge."
rule:
  kind: call_expression
  has:
    kind: simple_identifier
    regex: '^runBlocking$'
goodExample: suspend fun fetch(): Response = provider.complete(req)

yaml:
id: no-inline-coroutinescope-launch
language: kotlin
severity: error
message: "Launching on a throwaway CoroutineScope(...) leaks the scope — there is no reference left to cancel it."
note: "Hold the CoroutineScope as a field tied to a lifecycle (and cancel it), or use coroutineScope { }/supervisorScope { }."
rule:
  any:
    - pattern: CoroutineScope($$$ARGS).launch
    - pattern: CoroutineScope($$$ARGS).async

id: no-suspend-fun-returning-flow
language: kotlin
severity: warning
message: "A `suspend fun` returning a cold `Flow` is redundant and confusing; drop `suspend` (the Flow is already lazy) or return the value the caller actually needs."
rule:
  kind: function_declaration
  all:
    - has:
        kind: modifiers
        has:
          kind: function_modifier
          regex: '^suspend$'
    - has:
        kind: user_type
        all:
          - has:
              kind: type_identifier
              regex: '^Flow$'
          - follows:
              kind: function_value_parameters
              stopBy: end
badExample: suspend fun events(): Flow<Event> = flow { emitAll(source) }
goodExample: fun events(): Flow<Event> = flow { emitAll(source) }

yaml:
id: no-channel-unlimited
language: kotlin
severity: warning
message: "`Channel.UNLIMITED` removes all backpressure; an unbounded buffer can grow without limit. Use a bounded capacity with an explicit `BufferOverflow` policy."
rule:
  kind: navigation_expression
  pattern: Channel.UNLIMITED
goodExample: val ch = Channel<Event>(capacity = 64, onBufferOverflow = BufferOverflow.SUSPEND)

yaml:
id: no-flowon-main
language: kotlin
severity: warning
message: "`flowOn(Dispatchers.Main)` forces the flow's upstream onto the UI thread inside library code. Let the collector choose the context, or inject a dispatcher."
rule:
  pattern: $X.flowOn(Dispatchers.Main)
goodExample: upstream // emit on the collector's context; let the caller choose
withContext(ioContext) { fetch() } // ioContext: CoroutineContext = Dispatchers.Default

yaml:
id: no-print-stack-trace
language: kotlin
severity: error
rule:
  pattern: $E.printStackTrace()

id: no-console-output-in-library
language: kotlin
severity: error
rule:
  any:
    - pattern: println($$$A)
    - pattern: print($$$A)
    - pattern: System.out
    - pattern: System.err
goodExample: logger.debug { "received chunk $chunk" }

yaml:
id: prefer-typed-error-over-generic-throw
language: kotlin
severity: warning
rule:
  any:
    - pattern: throw Exception($$$A)
    - pattern: throw RuntimeException($$$A)
    - pattern: throw IllegalStateException($$$A)
    - pattern: throw Error($$$A)
goodExample: throw OpenAIProviderNotConfiguredError("model not configured")

yaml:
id: avoid-broad-catch-exception
language: kotlin
severity: warning
rule:
  kind: catch_block
  has:
    kind: user_type
    has:
      kind: type_identifier
      regex: '^Exception$'

id: no-todo-in-source
language: kotlin
severity: error
rule:
  any:
    - pattern: TODO()
    - pattern: TODO($MSG)
badExample: fun decode(text: String): List<T> = TODO("wire this up")
goodExample: fun decode(text: String): List<T> = aiSdkOutputJson.decodeFromString(serializer, text)

yaml:
id: prefer-typed-error-over-error-call
language: kotlin
severity: warning
rule:
  pattern: error($MSG)
constraints:
  MSG:
    has:
      kind: string_literal
badExample: return when (element) { is JsonObject -> element["result"]?.jsonPrimitive?.contentOrNull; else -> null } ?: error("Expected a JSON object with a `result` string")
goodExample: return when (element) { is JsonObject -> element["result"]?.jsonPrimitive?.contentOrNull; else -> null } ?: throw AiSdkException("Expected a JSON object with a `result` string")

yaml:
id: prefer-typed-error-over-checknotnull
language: kotlin
severity: warning
rule:
  pattern: checkNotNull($$$ARGS)
badExample: val composite = registry.lookup(id); val tracer = checkNotNull(composite) { "tracer missing" }
goodExample: val tracer = registry.lookup(id) ?: throw AiSdkException("no tracer registered for $id")

yaml:
id: no-let-elvis-run-pyramid
language: kotlin
severity: warning
rule:
  pattern: "$RECV?.let { $$$THEN } ?: run { $$$ELSE }"
goodExample: if (session == null) { startFresh(); notifyMiss() } else { resume(session) }

yaml:
id: no-java-import
language: kotlin
severity: error
rule:
  pattern: import java.$$$
goodExample: // Use an expect/actual or a pure-Kotlin primitive (see Crypto.kt) instead of importing java.*

yaml:
id: no-thread-sleep
language: kotlin
severity: error
rule:
  pattern: Thread.sleep($$$)
goodExample: delay(100) // kotlinx.coroutines.delay, suspending and multiplatform

yaml:
id: no-system-clock-env
language: kotlin
severity: error
rule:
  any:
    - pattern: System.currentTimeMillis()
    - pattern: System.nanoTime()
    - pattern: System.getenv($$$)
    - pattern: System.getProperty($$$)
goodExample: val t = Clock.System.now().toEpochMilliseconds() // kotlinx.datetime

yaml:
id: no-fq-java-time-uuid-date
language: kotlin
severity: error
rule:
  any:
    - pattern: java.util.UUID
    - pattern: java.util.Date
    - pattern: java.time.$$$
goodExample: val id = randomUuidV4() // pure-Kotlin/expect-actual id generator

yaml:
id: no-string-format
language: kotlin
severity: error
rule:
  pattern: String.format($$$)
goodExample: val s = "$count items" // string template

yaml:
id: no-jvm-synchronized
language: kotlin
severity: error
rule:
  any:
    - pattern: 'synchronized($LOCK) { $$$ }'
    - kind: annotation
      regex: '^@Synchronized$'
goodExample: mutex.withLock { counter++ } // kotlinx.coroutines.sync.Mutex

yaml:
id: prefer-kotlin-math
language: kotlin
severity: warning
rule:
  pattern: Math.$MEMBER
goodExample: val m = max(a, b) // import kotlin.math.max

yaml:
id: no-inline-json-instance
language: kotlin
severity: warning
rule:
  kind: call_expression
  all:
    - has:
        kind: simple_identifier
        regex: '^Json$'
    - has:
        kind: call_suffix
        has:
          kind: annotated_lambda
    - inside:
        kind: function_body
        stopBy: end
goodExample:
private val fooJson = Json { ignoreUnknownKeys = true }

fun decode(text: String): Foo = fooJson.decodeFromString(text)

yaml:
id: no-open-serializable-polymorphic-base
language: kotlin
severity: warning
rule:
  kind: class_declaration
  has:
    kind: modifiers
    all:
      - has:
          kind: inheritance_modifier
          regex: '^open$'
      - has:
          kind: annotation
          has:
            kind: user_type
            has:
              kind: type_identifier
              regex: '^Serializable$'
            stopBy: end

id: no-mutable-collection-in-public-fun
language: kotlin
severity: error
rule:
  kind: function_declaration
  all:
    - has:
        kind: modifiers
        has:
          kind: visibility_modifier
          regex: '^public$'
    - any:
        - has:
            kind: user_type
            all:
              - has:
                  kind: type_identifier
                  regex: '^Mutable(List|Map|Set)$'
                  stopBy: end
              - not:
                  precedes:
                    regex: '^\.$'
        - has:
            kind: function_value_parameters
            has:
              kind: parameter
              has:
                kind: user_type
                has:
                  kind: type_identifier
                  regex: '^Mutable(List|Map|Set)$'
                  stopBy: end
badExample: public suspend fun addClientAuthentication(headers: MutableMap<String, String>, params: MutableMap<String, String>): Unit = Unit
goodExample: public suspend fun addClientAuthentication(headers: Map<String, String>, params: Map<String, String>): Map<String, String> = headers + params

yaml:
id: prefer-list-over-array-return-in-public-fun
language: kotlin
severity: warning
rule:
  kind: function_declaration
  all:
    - has:
        kind: modifiers
        has:
          kind: visibility_modifier
          regex: '^public$'
    - has:
        kind: user_type
        all:
          - has:
              kind: type_identifier
              regex: '^Array$'
              stopBy: end
          - not:
              precedes:
                regex: '^\.$'
goodExample: public fun toolNames(): List<String> = names.toList()

yaml:
id: prefer-value-class-over-typealias-primitive
language: kotlin
severity: warning
rule:
  kind: type_alias
  has:
    kind: user_type
    has:
      kind: type_identifier
      regex: '^(Int|Long|Boolean|Double|Float|Char|Short|Byte|UInt|ULong|UShort|UByte)$'
message: "typealias to a primitive gives no type safety (every Int is assignable to it); prefer a value class (@JvmInline value class) to make illegal states unrepresentable."
badExample:
public typealias TokenCount = Int
typealias Millis = Long
goodExample:
@JvmInline public value class TokenCount(public val value: Int)
@JvmInline public value class Millis(public val value: Long)

yaml:
id: no-json-container-force-cast
language: kotlin
severity: error
rule:
  any:
    - pattern: $X as JsonObject
    - pattern: $X as JsonArray
badExample:
val obj = response as JsonObject
val items = response["data"] as JsonArray
goodExample:
val obj = decoder.obj(response, provider, op, "root")
val items = decoder.arr(obj["data"], provider, op, "data")

yaml:
id: no-keyed-json-container-cast
language: kotlin
severity: warning
rule:
  any:
    - pattern: $OBJ[$KEY] as? JsonObject
    - pattern: $OBJ[$KEY] as? JsonArray
badExample:
val messages = body["messages"] as? JsonArray
val fn = obj["function"] as? JsonObject
goodExample:
val req: ChatRequest = json.decodeFromJsonElement(body)
val messages = req.messages

yaml:
id: no-array-property-in-data-class
language: kotlin
severity: error
message: >-
  data class with an Array<T> primary-constructor property has structurally
  broken equals()/hashCode() (reference identity on the array). Use List<T>,
  or drop `data` and hand-write equals/hashCode.
rule:
  kind: class_declaration
  all:
    - has:
        kind: modifiers
        has:
          kind: class_modifier
          regex: '^data$'
    - has:
        kind: primary_constructor
        has:
          kind: class_parameter
          has:
            kind: user_type
            has:
              kind: type_identifier
              regex: '^Array$'
badExample: data class Embedding(val vector: Array<Float>, val model: String)
goodExample: data class Embedding(val vector: List<Float>, val model: String)

yaml:
id: no-primitive-array-property-in-data-class
language: kotlin
severity: error
message: >-
  data class with a primitive-array property (ByteArray/IntArray/...) has
  broken equals()/hashCode() (reference identity). Drop `data` and hand-write
  equals/hashCode (contentEquals), or wrap in a value type.
rule:
  kind: class_declaration
  all:
    - has:
        kind: modifiers
        has:
          kind: class_modifier
          regex: '^data$'
    - has:
        kind: primary_constructor
        has:
          kind: class_parameter
          has:
            kind: user_type
            has:
              kind: type_identifier
              regex: '^(ByteArray|IntArray|LongArray|ShortArray|CharArray|FloatArray|DoubleArray|BooleanArray)$'
badExample: private data class FalBinaryResponse(val bytes: ByteArray, val headers: Map<String, String>)
goodExample: private class FalBinaryResponse(val bytes: ByteArray, val headers: Map<String, String>)

yaml:
id: no-data-class-extending-throwable
language: kotlin
severity: error
message: >-
  Exceptions are identity objects, not value objects. A data class extending a
  Throwable gets copy() (fabricates a new exception with a fake stack/cause)
  and value equals(). Use a regular class; pattern-match on the sealed parent.
rule:
  kind: class_declaration
  all:
    - has:
        kind: modifiers
        has:
          kind: class_modifier
          regex: '^data$'
    - has:
        kind: delegation_specifier
        has:
          kind: constructor_invocation
          has:
            kind: user_type
            has:
              kind: type_identifier
              regex: '(Exception$|^Throwable$|^Error$|^AgentError$|^GatewayError$)'
badExample: public data class ToolExecution(val toolName: String, val cause: Throwable) : AgentError("...", cause)
goodExample: public class ToolExecution(public val toolName: String, cause: Throwable) : AgentError("...", cause)

yaml:
id: prefer-sealed-exception-over-open-class
language: kotlin
severity: warning
message: >-
  Prefer a sealed exception hierarchy over `open class : Exception/Throwable`.
  Sealed enables exhaustive `when` at catch sites; an open base lets arbitrary
  unknown subtypes escape the taxonomy. (Intentional extensibility? suppress.)
rule:
  kind: class_declaration
  all:
    - has:
        kind: modifiers
        has:
          kind: inheritance_modifier
          regex: '^open$'
    - has:
        kind: delegation_specifier
        has:
          kind: constructor_invocation
          has:
            kind: user_type
            has:
              kind: type_identifier
              regex: '^(Throwable|Exception|RuntimeException|Error)$'
badExample: public open class AiSdkException(message: String, cause: Throwable? = null) : RuntimeException(message, cause)
goodExample: public sealed class AiSdkException(message: String, cause: Throwable? = null) : RuntimeException(message, cause)

yaml:
id: no-float-equality-comparison
language: kotlin
severity: warning
message: >-
  ==/!= against a floating-point literal is fragile (rounding/representation
  error). Compare with a tolerance (abs(a - b) < epsilon), or check a typed
  range. (Exact 0.0 guard before division? acceptable — suppress locally.)
rule:
  kind: equality_expression
  has:
    kind: real_literal
goodExample: if (abs(temperature - 1.0) < 1e-9) { useDefault() }

yaml:
id: no-deferred-wiring-comment
language: kotlin
severity: error
# Catches the "declare the type surface now, wire the producer later" convention
# that the pipeline x-ray found behind half its data-flow defects: a public
# event/result/wire field is added with a silent default and a comment admitting
# it isn't populated yet ("wired ... as a follow-up", "type surface is in place",
# "the loop hasn't yet wired the field", "back-compat" null). Either wire the
# producer now or don't declare the field — never ship a permanently-empty seam.
rule:
  any:
    - kind: line_comment
    - kind: multiline_comment
  regex: '(?i)(wired|populate[ds]?) .{0,40}(later|follow-?up)|staged in as a follow-?up|type surface is in place|loop hasn.t yet wired|hasn.t yet wired the field|wire[ds]? the field'

badExample: public data class OnFooEvent(val x: Int)

yaml:
id: no-camelcase-top-level-function
language: kotlin
severity: error
rule:
  all:
    - kind: function_declaration
    - inside: { kind: source_file }
    - has: { kind: simple_identifier, regex: '^[a-z]', stopBy: neighbor }
    # `override` cannot appear on a genuine top-level function, but the PreToolUse
    # hook scans edit snippets in isolation where a member override parses at
    # source_file level — exclude it so the gate is snippet-safe (AR-15 misfire).
    - not:
        has:
          kind: modifiers
          has: { kind: member_modifier, regex: '^override$', stopBy: end }
goodExample: public fun GenerateText(): Unit = Unit

yaml:
id: no-empty-string-sentinel
language: kotlin
severity: warning
rule:
  pattern: '$X ?: ""'
goodExample: val content = maybeText ?: fallbackText

yaml:
id: no-jsonnull-sentinel
language: kotlin
severity: warning
rule:
  pattern: '$X ?: JsonNull'
goodExample: val value = element ?: JsonObject(emptyMap())

yaml:
id: no-lateinit-var
language: kotlin
severity: error
rule:
  all:
    - kind: property_declaration
    - has: { kind: member_modifier, regex: "^lateinit$", stopBy: end }
goodExample: var client: HttpClient? = null

yaml:
id: no-mutable-companion-state
language: kotlin
severity: error
# Mutable `var` PROPERTY of a companion object = process-wide shared mutable singleton state.
# Match only DIRECT companion properties (immediate parent is the companion's class_body) — the
# previous `inside: companion_object, stopBy: end` over-matched, flagging function-local `var`s
# inside companion METHODS (a false positive: locals are not shared state). Mirrors the correct
# immediate-parent form of no-var-in-object-declaration.
rule:
  all:
    - kind: property_declaration
    - has: { kind: binding_pattern_kind, regex: "^var$" }
    - inside:
        kind: class_body
        stopBy: neighbor
        inside: { kind: companion_object, stopBy: neighbor }

id: no-not-null-assertion
language: kotlin
severity: error
rule:
  pattern: '$X!!'
goodExample: val value = requireNotNull(maybe)

yaml:
id: no-nullable-prompt-messages-bag
language: kotlin
severity: error
rule:
  any:
    - pattern: require($A != null || $B.isNotEmpty())
    - pattern: 'require($A != null || $B.isNotEmpty()) { $$$MSG }'
goodExample: require(prompt != null) { "prompt is required" }

yaml:
id: no-provideroptions-jsonobject-cast
language: kotlin
severity: warning
rule:
  any:
    - pattern: providerOptions[$KEY] as? JsonObject
    - pattern: providerOptions[$KEY] as JsonObject
goodExample: val options = providerOptions["openai"]?.jsonObject

yaml:
id: no-sealed-interface
language: kotlin
severity: error
# Tenet: a sealed hierarchy's root is a sealed CLASS, not a sealed interface — EXCEPT two
# carve-outs the user approved (2026-06-22):
#   - `@Serializable` polymorphic wire types — sealed interface is the idiomatic kotlinx
#     pattern (a variant can be a data class/object that also implements other interfaces).
#   - `private` sealed interfaces — internal state machines, not public API.
#
# The previous pattern-based form (`pattern: sealed interface $NAME`, …) had a GAP: it
# missed sealed interfaces carrying annotations/visibility modifiers, so it silently passed
# 5 real cases in commonMain (caught by the Konsist whole-codebase layer). This kind+has rule
# matches a class_declaration that (a) is an interface (`interface` keyword child) and (b)
# carries the `sealed` class_modifier, then carves out @Serializable + private.
rule:
  all:
    - kind: class_declaration
    - has: { regex: '^interface$', stopBy: neighbor }
    - has:
        kind: modifiers
        has: { kind: class_modifier, regex: '^sealed$' }
  not:
    any:
      - has: { kind: modifiers, has: { kind: annotation, regex: 'Serializable' } }
      - has: { kind: modifiers, has: { kind: visibility_modifier, regex: '^private$' } }
goodExample: private sealed interface StreamEvent

yaml:
id: no-secondary-constructor
language: kotlin
severity: error
rule:
  kind: secondary_constructor
goodExample: class Client(val name: String)

yaml:
id: no-top-level-mutable-var
language: kotlin
severity: error
rule:
  all:
    - kind: property_declaration
    - has: { kind: binding_pattern_kind, regex: "^var$" }
    - inside: { kind: source_file }
goodExample: public val globalState: String = ""

yaml:
id: no-typealias-string
language: kotlin
severity: warning
rule:
  any:
    - pattern: public typealias $NAME = String
    - pattern: typealias $NAME = String
goodExample:
@JvmInline
value class UserId(val value: String)
